text: Artificial intelligence is becoming more common in everyday life, but its use raises important ethical questions. One major concern is bias. If AI systems are trained on unfair data, they can make biased decisions, especially in areas like hiring or policing. Fairness means ensuring these systems treat all people equally, regardless of their background. Another key issue is privacy. AI often needs large amounts of personal data, so we must protect this information from misuse. Transparency is also crucial—users should understand how an AI reaches its conclusions. For these reasons, responsible AI development focuses on creating technology that is ethical, trustworthy, and beneficial for everyone.
